metadata.task: Determine which reaches the bottom first when released on an inclined plane.
metadata.description: Work from the Rolling Cylinder vs Sliding Block scene state rather than from the title alone. Query accessible object parameters and, when the task involves motion, collisions, forces, or oscillation, step the simulator and compare measured states before submitting the numeric answer.

expected_behavior: The LLM observes both objects moving down the ramp and determines which reaches the bottom first
reasoning: A sliding block reaches the bottom first because all gravitational potential energy converts to translational kinetic energy. A rolling cylinder must split energy between translational and rotational motion, making it slower.
